originalBehavior: The published article loads through a shared article shell plus the page-local base and airfoil scripts, then initializes many inline flow-visualization, particle, pressure, viscosity, and airfoil scenes under one longform page.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published article structure, shared article styling, route-local model data, and both vendored scripts while rewriting static-hosting metadata and asset paths for the subpath-hosted replica.
notes: The public body no longer exposes the upstream author banner, blog navigation, analytics, or footer supporter prompt.
The route keeps the original longform article structure instead of condensing the content into a custom local rewrite.
evidence: Implementation planning treated the published article shell, the local airfoil model data, and both scripts as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the route loads locally, both scripts initialize, and the opening, drag, viscosity, and later airfoil scenes remain interactive.

originalBehavior: The published article opens with a slider-driven airfoil flow scene and then steps through grass, marker, and particle visualizations, including draggable 3D particle views.
localStatus: The local route keeps the opening angle-of-attack scene, early flow-visualization sections, and draggable particle views under the original published runtime.
notes: The smoke contract treats one opening slider path and one dragable particle scene as required parity behavior.
The route preserves the published progression from flow visualization into particle-level reasoning instead of flattening those sections into static diagrams.
evidence: Post-bicycle rollout planning called for one flow-speed or angle-control path, one drag-based viewing scene, and responsive-shell validation.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the opening hero scene updates from slider input and a particle scene responds to drag.

originalBehavior: The published article continues through viscosity, boundary-layer, pressure, lift, and later airfoil-shape sections with linked pressure fields, markers, and angle-of-attack controls.
localStatus: The local route keeps the viscosity, pressure, and later airfoil-shape scenes intact through the original article canvas and slider runtime.
notes: The published CFD-style scenes now load their shared airfoil geometry from the route-local model tree instead of the upstream root path.
The shipped `sound` and `bicycle` references are rewritten locally in the public body.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one viscosity or pressure scene and one later airfoil or plate scene as parity-critical for the airfoil batch.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the viscosity scene and a later airfoil-shape scene update from slider input without runtime asset failures.

originalBehavior: The published article closes with further-reading sections and an upstream footer that includes supporter messaging and author provenance, while also referring back to other Bartosz articles by upstream URL.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published closing sections while leaving the shared footer as the only public original-page reference surface and rewriting available local cross-links.
notes: The local copy uses local fallback font stacks so the route does not depend on runtime Google Fonts requests.
The public body no longer includes the Patreon footer prompt or other author-promo surfaces.
evidence: The hardened Ciechanowski audit flags ordinary in-body upstream article links while allowing footer-only provenance.
Route cleanup rewrote the local `sound` and `bicycle` references so the family continues converging toward local intra-family navigation.
